pages.text: The $(item)Hamlet Skyseed$() — planks, cobblestone and an $(item)emerald$() (mine a Rocky mountain for emeralds). A villager's cottage — the start of trading.
Everything's in hand — here's how it goes together.

A little cottage on a grassy isle, home to one villager — the seed of a whole trading economy. The first of the village islands. The cottage comes in a few styles, each randomly turned and weathered, so no two hamlets look quite alike.

$(bold)Put them to work$()$(br)The villager arrives $(item)unemployed$(). Set a job-site block beside them — a $(item)composter$(), $(item)lectern$(), $(item)cauldron$(), and so on — and they take up that trade. They dress to match the biome you grew them over.

$(bold)A home$()$(br)The cottage has a bed they will claim, so they restock and can breed (add more beds and food). $(br)Raids are switched off on Skyseed worlds — illagers would only path into the void — but $(item)iron golems$() still appear once a village grows large enough.

$(bold)A home gone wrong$()$(br)Rarely a Hamlet grows $(o)abandoned$() instead: a cobwebbed ruin with no bed and a $(o)zombie villager$() for a tenant. Cure them and you have a villager — often with a discount.
